docs.title: sc4py
docs.text:
Utilities for date/time, environment parsing, dynamic class loading, percentages, string-to-bool conversion, choice tuples, and in-memory ZIP reading.

Package source

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install sc4py

docs.title: Modules
docs.text: Module Purpose sc4py.choice Convert plain values and Enum types to Django-style choice tuples sc4py.datetime Date/time helpers and daterange generator sc4py.env Type-safe environment variable reading sc4py.klass Dynamic class instantiation from dotted path strings sc4py.number Percentage calculation sc4py.str str2bool — multilingual boolean string parsing sc4py.zip In-memory ZIP extraction (text and CSV)

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
from sc4py.datetime import now_str, daterange
from sc4py.env import env_as_bool
from sc4py.number import percentage
from sc4py.str import str2bool
from datetime import date

print(now_str())                                    # "20-04-2026 14:30:00"
print(env_as_bool("FEATURE_ENABLED", "true"))       # True
print(percentage(45, 60))                           # 75.0
print(str2bool("sim"))                              # True

for d in daterange(date(2026, 1, 1), date(2026, 1, 3)):
    print(d)

# 2026-01-01
# 2026-01-02
# 2026-01-03

docs.title: sc4py.choice
docs.text: Convert plain values and Enum types into Django-style (value, label) choice tuples.

docs.title: to_choice(*args) → list[tuple[Any, Any]]
docs.text:
Accepts any mix of plain values, Enum classes, or Enum instances and returns a flat list of (value, label) pairs.

Input type Value Label Plain value the value itself the value itself Enum class each member's .value .description if present, else .value Enum instance .value .description if present, else .value

docs.title: Plain values
docs.text:
from sc4py.choice import to_choice

to_choice("active", "inactive")
# [("active", "active"), ("inactive", "inactive")]

to_choice("x", 2)
# [("x", "x"), (2, 2)]

docs.title: Plain Enum
docs.text:
from enum import Enum
from sc4py.choice import to_choice

class Status(Enum):
    ACTIVE = 1
    INACTIVE = 2

to_choice(Status)
# [(1, 1), (2, 2)]

docs.title: Enum with descriptions
docs.text:
from enum import Enum
from sc4py.choice import to_choice

class Priority(Enum):
    description: str
    LOW = (1, "Low priority")
    HIGH = (2, "High priority")

    def __new__(cls, value, description):
        obj = object.__new__(cls)
        obj._value_ = value
        obj.description = description
        return obj

to_choice(Priority)
# [(1, "Low priority"), (2, "High priority")]

to_choice(Priority.HIGH)
# [(2, "High priority")]

docs.title: Mix of types
docs.text:
to_choice("none", Status, Priority.HIGH)
# [("none", "none"), (1, 1), (2, 2), (2, "High priority")]

docs.title: sc4py.datetime
docs.text: Date and time helpers plus a daterange generator.

docs.title: Aliases
docs.text: Name Aliased to today datetime.date.today now datetime.datetime.now

docs.title: now_str() → str
docs.text:
Returns the current local datetime as "DD-MM-YYYY HH:MM:SS".

from sc4py.datetime import now_str

print(now_str())  # "20-04-2026 14:30:00"

docs.title: this_month() → int
docs.text:
Returns the current month as an integer (1–12).

from sc4py.datetime import this_month

print(this_month())  # 4

docs.title: others_months() → list[int]
docs.text:
Returns all months except the current one.

from sc4py.datetime import others_months

# If current month is April (4):
others_months()
# [1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12]

docs.title: daterange(start, end, step=timedelta(1)) → Generator[date, None, None]
docs.text:
Yields every date from start to end inclusive, advancing by step.

Parameter Type Default Description start date — First date (inclusive) end date — Last date (inclusive) step timedelta timedelta(1) Interval between dates 
from datetime import date, timedelta
from sc4py.datetime import daterange

# Daily
for d in daterange(date(2026, 1, 1), date(2026, 1, 3)):
    print(d)
# 2026-01-01
# 2026-01-02
# 2026-01-03

# Every 7 days
for d in daterange(date(2026, 1, 1), date(2026, 1, 22), timedelta(weeks=1)):
    print(d)
# 2026-01-01
# 2026-01-08
# 2026-01-15
# 2026-01-22

docs.title: sc4py.env
docs.text:
Type-safe helpers for reading environment variables.

All functions share an optional wrapped parameter: when True, leading and trailing single-quotes are stripped from the value (useful for values set as 'foo' in shell scripts).

docs.title: env(name, default=None, wrapped=False) → str | None
docs.text:
Reads an environment variable as a raw string.

import os
from sc4py.env import env

os.environ["APP_NAME"] = "sc4py"
env("APP_NAME")           # "sc4py"
env("MISSING", "default") # "default"
env("MISSING")            # None

docs.title: env_as_list(name, default="", delimiter=",", wrapped=False) → list[str] | None
docs.text:
Splits the variable value by delimiter.

import os
from sc4py.env import env_as_list

os.environ["ALLOWED_HOSTS"] = "localhost,127.0.0.1,example.com"
env_as_list("ALLOWED_HOSTS")
# ["localhost", "127.0.0.1", "example.com"]

env_as_list("MISSING")   # []
env_as_list("MISSING", default=None)  # None

docs.title: env_as_list_of_maps(name, key, default="", delimiter=",", wrapped=False) → list[dict] | None
docs.text:
Same as env_as_list but wraps each item in a dict with key.

import os
from sc4py.env import env_as_list_of_maps

os.environ["SERVERS"] = "host1,host2"
env_as_list_of_maps("SERVERS", "host")
# [{"host": "host1"}, {"host": "host2"}]

docs.title: env_as_bool(name, default=None, wrapped=False) → bool | None
docs.text:
Parses the variable value via str2bool.

import os
from sc4py.env import env_as_bool

os.environ["DEBUG"] = "true"
env_as_bool("DEBUG")           # True

os.environ["DEBUG"] = "false"
env_as_bool("DEBUG")           # False

env_as_bool("MISSING")         # None
env_as_bool("MISSING", "true") # True

docs.title: env_as_int(name, default=None, wrapped=False) → int | None
docs.text:
Parses the variable value as an integer.

import os
from sc4py.env import env_as_int

os.environ["PORT"] = "8080"
env_as_int("PORT")      # 8080
env_as_int("MISSING")   # None

docs.title: env_from_json(name, default="", wrapped=False) → dict | list | None
docs.text:
Parses the variable value as JSON.

import os
from sc4py.env import env_from_json

os.environ["CONFIG"] = '{"debug": true, "port": 8080}'
env_from_json("CONFIG")
# {"debug": True, "port": 8080}

docs.title: sc4py.klass
docs.text: Dynamic class instantiation from dotted-path strings.

docs.title: instantiate_class(full_class_name, *args, **kwargs) → object
docs.text:
Imports the module and class identified by full_class_name and returns a new instance, forwarding any extra positional or keyword arguments to the constructor.

Parameter Type Description full_class_name str Dotted path: "module.submodule.ClassName" *args Any Positional arguments forwarded to __init__ **kwargs Any Keyword arguments forwarded to __init__ 
from sc4py.klass import instantiate_class

# Equivalent to: from decimal import Decimal; Decimal("3.14")
d = instantiate_class("decimal.Decimal", "3.14")
print(d)        # Decimal('3.14')
print(type(d))  # <class 'decimal.Decimal'>

from sc4py.klass import instantiate_class

# Instantiate a custom class from application code
svc = instantiate_class("myapp.services.EmailService", host="smtp.example.com", port=587)

Note

full_class_name must contain at least one dot separating the module path from the class name.

docs.title: sc4py.number
docs.text: Numeric helpers.

docs.title: percentage(num1, num2, precision=2) → float
docs.text:
Returns num1 / num2 * 100 rounded to precision decimal places. Returns 0.0 if either argument is zero.

Parameter Type Default Description num1 float — Numerator num2 float — Denominator precision int 2 Decimal places in the result 
from sc4py.number import percentage

percentage(45, 60)         # 75.0
percentage(1, 3)           # 33.33
percentage(1, 3, precision=4)  # 33.3333
percentage(0, 100)         # 0.0
percentage(100, 0)         # 0.0

docs.title: sc4py.str
docs.text: String conversion helpers.

docs.title: str2bool(v) → bool | None
docs.text:
Converts a boolean-like value to True, False, or None.

Parameter Type Description v str \| bool \| int \| None Value to convert

Returns True for:

"true", "verdade", "yes", "sim", "t", "v", "y", "s", "1", 1, True

Returns False for:

"false", "falso", "no", "nao", "não", "f", "n", "0", 0, False

Returns None for:

None, "", " "

Raises ValueError for any other value.

Matching is case-insensitive and strips surrounding whitespace.

from sc4py.str import str2bool

str2bool("true")    # True
str2bool("sim")     # True   (Portuguese)
str2bool("yes")     # True
str2bool(1)         # True

str2bool("false")   # False
str2bool("não")     # False  (Portuguese)
str2bool(0)         # False

str2bool(None)      # None
str2bool("")        # None

str2bool("maybe")   # raises ValueError

docs.title: sc4py.zip
docs.text: In-memory ZIP extraction helpers — read text files and CSV files directly from raw bytes without writing to disk.

docs.title: FileNotFoundInZipError
docs.text: Subclass of FileNotFoundError. Raised when the requested file cannot be found inside the ZIP archive.

docs.title: unzip_content(content, file_id=0, encoding="utf-8") → str | bytes
docs.text:
Extracts a single file from a ZIP archive provided as bytes.

Parameter Type Default Description content bytes — Raw ZIP bytes file_id int \| str 0 Index (int) or filename (str) of the entry to extract encoding str \| None "utf-8" Encoding used to decode the bytes; pass None to return raw bytes 
import requests
from sc4py.zip import unzip_content

raw = requests.get("https://example.com/data.zip").content

# First file by index
text = unzip_content(raw)

# Specific file by name
text = unzip_content(raw, file_id="report.txt")

# Raw bytes (no decoding)
data = unzip_content(raw, encoding=None)

docs.title: unzip_csv_content(content, file_id=0, encoding="utf-8", **kwargs) → list[dict]
docs.text:
Extracts a CSV file from a ZIP archive and returns it as a list of dicts (one per row).

Extra keyword arguments are forwarded to csv.DictReader (e.g. delimiter, fieldnames).

Parameter Type Default Description content bytes — Raw ZIP bytes file_id int \| str 0 Index or filename of the CSV entry encoding str "utf-8" Encoding of the CSV text **kwargs Forwarded to csv.DictReader 
from sc4py.zip import unzip_csv_content

with open("archive.zip", "rb") as f:
    rows = unzip_csv_content(f.read(), file_id="sales.csv")

for row in rows:
    print(row)
# {"date": "2026-01-01", "amount": "100.00"}
# ...

# Custom delimiter
rows = unzip_csv_content(raw, file_id="data.txt", delimiter=";")
